checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is block RLP size validated before any processing occurs (EIP-7934)?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify the entry point where blocks are received from P2P network. 2. Verify that len(rlp.encode(block)) > MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE check occurs BEFORE any RLP decoding or block header parsing. 3. Craft a block payload exceeding MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE and verify rejection at the network layer. 4. Confirm that oversized blocks do not trigger resource-intensive operations before rejection.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-1, Trust Boundary: tb-010. EIP-7934 specific defense. A P2P peer can send arbitrarily large block payloads.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are blocks with non-empty ommers rejected in post-merge Ethereum?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Create a block with block.ommers set to a non-empty tuple. 2. Submit block via P2P network. 3. Verify that state_transition rejects the block with an appropriate error. 4. Verify that validate_header also enforces this constraint (defense-in-depth).
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-2, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Post-merge invariant. Non-empty ommers could trigger pre-merge code paths.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Do all 8 computed roots/hashes match header values after block execution?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. For each of the 8 fields (gas_used, transactions_root, state_root, receipt_root, bloom, withdrawals_root, blob_gas_used, requests_hash), create a block with a mismatched computed vs header value. 2. Verify that state_transition rejects each case. 3. Check that comparison is performed with constant-time equality for cryptographic hashes. 4. Verify no early-exit on first mismatch allows partial state to be committed.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-3, Trust Boundaries: tb-002, tb-005. Bypass allows invalid state transitions leading to consensus failure.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is transaction sender identity correctly derived from ECDSA signature?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify recover_sender correctly implements ECDSA recovery from (v, r, s) signature. 2. Test with malleable signatures (high-s values) and verify rejection per EIP-2. 3. Test edge cases: s=0, r=0, v outside valid range, points on curve boundaries. 4. Attempt signature forgery using known attack vectors and verify detection.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-5, Trust Boundary: tb-001. Signature spoofing allows account takeover.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is RLP encoding deterministic and collision-free?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Test RLP encoding with various block structures and verify determinism. 2. Verify that for B1 != B2, rlp.encode(B1) != rlp.encode(B2). 3. Test with nested structures, empty fields, and maximum-size payloads. 4. Cross-check encoding output against reference implementations.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-6, Trust Boundaries: tb-002, tb-005, tb-010. Non-deterministic encoding causes consensus splits.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is keccak256 collision-resistant and correctly implemented?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify keccak256 implementation produces correct test vectors. 2. Check for any implementation-specific optimizations that might introduce bugs. 3. Verify hash comparison uses constant-time equality. 4. Test with edge cases: empty input, single byte, maximum size inputs.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-7, Trust Boundaries: tb-002, tb-005. Implementation bug in hash computation could enable block forgery.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is withdrawal amount scaled correctly by 10^9 (Gwei to Wei)?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Trace the process_withdrawals function to verify the scaling factor of 10^9. 2. Test with withdrawal amounts at boundary values (0, max uint64 Gwei). 3. Verify no overflow occurs during multiplication. 4. Confirm balance increase exactly equals withdrawal.amount * 10^9 Wei.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-8, Trust Boundaries: tb-003, tb-007. Incorrect scaling affects total ETH supply.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does withdrawal processing only increase balances, never decrease?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Review process_withdrawals implementation for any subtraction operations. 2. Test for integer overflow that could cause wraparound to lower values. 3. Verify balance_after(a) >= balance_before(a) for all accounts a. 4. Test with maximum balance values approaching overflow boundaries.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-9, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Balance decrease during withdrawal is fund loss.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Do system contracts contain code before checked system transactions execute?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify process_checked_system_transaction checks len(system_contract_code) > 0. 2. Test at fork boundary to ensure system contracts are deployed. 3. Verify InvalidBlock is raised when contract has no code. 4. Note: unchecked variants (SG-010) do not have this check.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-10, Trust Boundary: tb-004. Missing system contract code causes block processing failure.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are checked system transaction errors propagated as InvalidBlock?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify process_checked_system_transaction raises InvalidBlock if system_tx_output.error is not None. 2. Identify ways to cause system contract reversion (WITHDRAWAL_REQUEST_ADDRESS, CONSOLIDATION_REQUEST_ADDRESS). 3. Verify that error check cannot be bypassed. 4. Test what happens if system contract can be made to revert on every block.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-11, Trust Boundaries: tb-004, tb-011. If system contract reverts on every block, chain halts.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are unchecked system transactions only used for beacon roots and history storage?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify that process_unchecked_system_transaction is only called for BEACON_ROOTS_ADDRESS and HISTORY_STORAGE_ADDRESS. 2. Confirm that failure of these contracts is non-critical (affects ring buffer data only). 3. Verify a compromised CL cannot corrupt critical state via unchecked system transactions.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-12, Trust Boundary: tb-004. Unchecked variants allow silent failures for non-critical operations.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Do all blob versioned hashes have 0x01 version prefix?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit blob transaction with versioned hashes having non-0x01 prefix (e.g., 0x00, 0x02). 2. Verify rejection at transaction validation stage. 3. Verify EL only checks version byte; KZG proof verification is by CL. 4. Test with hashes that have correct prefix but invalid KZG commitments.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-15, Trust Boundary: tb-008. Non-KZG versioned hash breaks data availability guarantees.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is gas refund capped at gas_used // 5 (EIP-3529)?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Craft transaction with operations generating large refund counters (e.g., SSTORE clearing). 2. Verify tx_gas_refund = min(gas_used // 5, refund_counter). 3. Test boundary cases where refund_counter exceeds gas_used // 5. 4. Verify refund never exceeds 20% of gas consumed.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-16, Trust Boundary: tb-006. Excess refunds enable spam at reduced cost.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is EIP-7623 calldata floor gas cost enforced after refund?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Craft transaction with large calldata and refund-generating operations. 2. Verify tx_gas_used = max(gas_used - refund, calldata_floor_gas_cost). 3. Test that final gas used is at least the calldata floor. 4. Test boundary cases where refunds would push below floor.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-17, Trust Boundary: tb-006. Bypassing floor allows cheap data availability exploitation.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are post-merge header fields (difficulty, nonce, ommers_hash) correctly zeroed?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Create block with non-zero difficulty, non-zero nonce, or non-EMPTY_OMMER_HASH ommers_hash. 2. Verify validate_header rejects each case. 3. Verify defense-in-depth: state_transition also enforces these constraints. 4. Check for client-specific divergence on pre-merge code paths.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-18, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Non-zero post-merge fields could trigger pre-merge code paths.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are sender accounts with code validated for EIP-7702 delegation designations?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit transaction from account with code that is NOT a valid delegation designation. 2. Verify check_transaction rejects the transaction. 3. Test EIP-7702 authorization list validation for bypass vectors. 4. Verify contracts cannot initiate unauthorized transactions.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-19, Trust Boundary: tb-009. Bypass allows unauthorized code execution over victim accounts.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is transaction gas checked against remaining block gas?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit transaction with tx.gas > (block_gas_limit - block_gas_used). 2. Verify check_transaction rejects the transaction. 3. Test at boundary: tx.gas exactly equals remaining gas. 4. Test for integer overflow in gas calculation.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-21, Trust Boundary: tb-001. Bypass causes block gas limit overrun and consensus failure.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is transaction blob gas checked against remaining block blob gas?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit blob transaction with calculate_total_blob_gas(tx) > blob_gas_available. 2. Verify check_transaction rejects the transaction. 3. Test for overflow in total_blob_gas calculation. 4. Test at exact boundary values.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-22, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-008. Bypass allows excess blob data breaking data availability assumptions.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is max_fee_per_gas >= max_priority_fee_per_gas enforced for EIP-1559 transactions?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit EIP-1559 transaction with max_priority_fee_per_gas > max_fee_per_gas. 2. Verify check_transaction rejects the transaction. 3. Test for arithmetic underflow in effective gas price calculation. 4. Test boundary case: priority fee exactly equals max fee.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-23, Trust Boundary: tb-001. Inconsistent fee structure could cause underflow.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is max_fee_per_gas >= base_fee_per_gas enforced?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit transaction with max_fee_per_gas < current base_fee_per_gas. 2. Verify check_transaction rejects the transaction. 3. Verify rejection occurs before execution, not after. 4. Test at exact boundary: max fee equals base fee.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-24, Trust Boundary: tb-001. Acceptance allows under-priced execution.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Do blob transactions have between 1 and BLOB_COUNT_LIMIT blobs?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit blob transaction with 0 blobs (empty blob_versioned_hashes). 2. Submit blob transaction with > MAX_BLOB_COUNT blobs. 3. Verify both cases are rejected by check_transaction. 4. Test at exact boundaries: 1 blob and MAX_BLOB_COUNT blobs.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-25, Trust Boundary: tb-008. Zero blobs wastes capacity; excess breaks data availability.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are blob and SetCode transactions prevented from creating contracts?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit blob transaction with tx.to = None (contract creation). 2. Submit SetCode transaction with tx.to = None. 3. Verify check_transaction rejects both cases. 4. Test for bypasses using special address values.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-26, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-008, tb-009. Allowing contract creation violates EIP semantics.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Do SetCode transactions require non-empty authorization lists?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit SetCode transaction with empty authorization list. 2. Verify check_transaction rejects the transaction. 3. Test for bypasses with malformed authorization entries. 4. Verify this indicates broader EIP-7702 validation coverage.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-27, Trust Boundary: tb-009. Empty list is semantically invalid.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does sender nonce match transaction nonce exactly?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit transaction with nonce < sender_account.nonce. 2. Submit transaction with nonce > sender_account.nonce. 3. Verify both are rejected by check_transaction. 4. Test for nonce verification bypass vectors enabling replay attacks.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-28, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-006. Nonce bypass enables replay or ordering attacks.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is sender balance sufficient for maximum possible transaction cost?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify balance check: sender_balance >= (tx.gas * max_fee_per_gas) + tx.value + (blob_gas * max_fee_per_blob_gas). 2. Test with extreme gas parameters near uint256 boundary to trigger overflow. 3. Test with sender balance exactly at required amount. 4. Verify no integer overflow in cost calculation.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-29, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-006. Bypass creates ETH from nothing -- critical fund loss.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is nonce incremented before message execution?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Trace process_transaction to verify increment_nonce(state, sender) is called before process_message_call. 2. Test for reentrancy scenarios where same nonce could be reused. 3. Verify ordering is correct for cross-transaction protection within same block.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-30, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-006. Late nonce increment enables reentrancy attacks.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are gas fees deducted from sender balance before message execution?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Trace process_transaction to verify max gas fees are deducted before process_message_call. 2. Test if sender can spend the balance during execution to avoid paying gas. 3. Verify both effective_gas_fee and blob_gas_fee are deducted upfront.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-31, Trust Boundary: tb-006. Late fee deduction enables fee-evasion exploit.

checklist.title: Is unused gas refunded at the correct effective_gas_price?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify refund calculation: gas_left * effective_gas_price. 2. Test for incorrect price usage (base fee vs effective price). 3. Calculate expected refund and compare to actual refund. 4. Test with various gas price configurations.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-32, Trust Boundary: tb-006. Incorrect refund affects ETH supply accuracy.

checklist.title: Does coinbase receive only the priority fee portion (not base fee)?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify coinbase payment: tx_gas_used * (effective_gas_price - base_fee_per_gas). 2. Confirm base fee is burned, not paid to coinbase. 3. Test for incorrect full payment of effective gas price. 4. Verify EIP-1559 deflationary mechanism is correctly implemented.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-33, Trust Boundary: tb-006. Base fee to coinbase breaks EIP-1559 economics.

checklist.title: Are accounts marked for deletion destroyed after transaction execution?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Trigger SELFDESTRUCT to mark account for deletion. 2. Verify account is destroyed after transaction execution. 3. Test if accounts_to_delete set is properly processed. 4. Verify state root reflects account deletion.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-34, Trust Boundary: tb-006. Failure to delete causes state trie divergence.

checklist.title: Does beacon roots system transaction execute before user transactions?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify apply_body calls process_unchecked_system_transaction(BEACON_ROOTS_ADDRESS) first. 2. Test if user transactions can access expected beacon root data via ring buffer. 3. Verify EIP-4788 dependent contracts work correctly.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-35, Trust Boundary: tb-004. Incorrect ordering causes beacon root unavailability.

checklist.title: Does history storage system transaction execute as second operation?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify apply_body calls process_unchecked_system_transaction(HISTORY_STORAGE_ADDRESS) second. 2. Test history data availability during transaction execution. 3. Verify contracts querying historical block hashes work correctly.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-36, Trust Boundary: tb-004. Incorrect ordering causes stale history data.

checklist.title: Are transactions processed in sequential order within a block?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify apply_body processes transactions in block transaction list order. 2. Test that out-of-order processing produces different state roots. 3. Verify sequential ordering is a consensus invariant. 4. Check for any parallelization that might violate ordering.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-37, Trust Boundary: tb-006. Out-of-order processing causes consensus failure.

checklist.title: Are withdrawals processed after all transactions?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify apply_body calls process_withdrawals after all transactions. 2. Test that premature withdrawal processing alters state visible to transactions. 3. Verify state root divergence if ordering is violated.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-38, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Premature processing causes state root divergence.

checklist.title: Are general purpose requests processed last in block body?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify apply_body calls process_general_purpose_requests after withdrawals and transactions. 2. Test that premature processing produces incorrect request data. 3. Verify requests_hash divergence if ordering is violated.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-39, Trust Boundary: tb-011. Incorrect ordering produces different requests_hash.

checklist.title: Are request types appended in correct order (deposits, withdrawals, consolidations)?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Craft transactions generating deposit logs, withdrawal requests, and consolidation requests. 2. Verify process_general_purpose_requests assembles in order: deposits, withdrawals, consolidations. 3. Test that incorrect ordering produces different requests_hash. 4. Verify consensus split if ordering differs between implementations.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-40, Trust Boundary: tb-011. Request ordering determines requests_hash.

checklist.title: Are deposit requests parsed from block output logs (not system transactions)?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify deposit requests are derived via parse_deposit_requests from log entries. 2. Test for incorrect contract address or ABI decoding. 3. Craft log entries attempting to inject fraudulent deposits. 4. Verify legitimate deposits are not missed.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-41, Trust Boundary: tb-011. Incorrect parsing could inject fraudulent validator deposits.

checklist.title: Are withdrawal and consolidation requests from checked system transactions?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify process_checked_system_transaction is called for withdrawal and consolidation request contracts. 2. Test what happens if checked is replaced with unchecked. 3. Manipulate system contract state to cause failures. 4. Verify failures are caught and not silently ignored.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-42, Trust Boundaries: tb-004, tb-011. Silent failures corrupt request data network-wide.

checklist.title: Are blocks with number < 1 rejected?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit block with header.number = 0 via P2P. 2. Verify validate_header rejects the block. 3. Test for chain state corruption if accepted. 4. Test for triggering genesis-specific code paths.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-43, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Block 0 could trigger unexpected code paths.

checklist.title: Are block timestamps strictly increasing?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit block with timestamp <= parent timestamp. 2. Verify validate_header rejects the block. 3. Test TIMESTAMP opcode behavior with non-increasing timestamps. 4. Verify time-locked contracts work correctly.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-44, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Non-increasing timestamps break time-dependent operations.

checklist.title: Are block numbers sequential (parent.number + 1)?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit block with number != parent.number + 1 (gap or duplicate). 2. Verify validate_header rejects the block. 3. Test for chain continuity breaks. 4. Test for block number reuse attacks.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-45, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Non-sequential numbers break chain continuity.

checklist.title: Does parent_hash cryptographically match parent header?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Create block with parent_hash != keccak256(rlp.encode(parent_header)). 2. Verify validate_header rejects the block. 3. Test for chain reorganization attacks. 4. Verify cryptographic hash verification is correct.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-47, Trust Boundaries: tb-002, tb-005. Mismatch enables chain reorganization attacks.

checklist.title: Does excess_blob_gas match calculation from parent?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit block with incorrect excess_blob_gas. 2. Verify validate_header rejects the block. 3. Test for arithmetic edge cases (underflow when target > used). 4. Verify client divergence on different arithmetic handling.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-48, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Incorrect value affects blob fee calculations.

checklist.title: Is gas_used <= gas_limit enforced in header?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit block with gas_used > gas_limit in header. 2. Verify validate_header rejects the block. 3. Test gas accounting consistency. 4. Verify consensus failure on gas_used root check.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-49, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Exceeding limit breaks gas accounting model.

checklist.title: Do system transactions originate from SYSTEM_ADDRESS (0xff...fe)?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify process_system_transaction sets origin and caller to SYSTEM_ADDRESS. 2. Test for non-SYSTEM_ADDRESS caller scenarios. 3. Verify system contracts have caller-based access controls. 4. Test if incorrect caller bypasses access controls.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-52, Trust Boundary: tb-004. Incorrect address allows non-privileged callers.
